Alright guys, welcome to theme plate 18. This is the Danny Tanner and for those of you who aren't familiar Danny Tanner was this fictional character played by Bob Sagan on the 90s show Full House who kind of became this caricature, this meme, you know, memes before memes were invented but he became famous for his timely pep talks at the end of an episode, you know, that usually resulted in acceptance forgiveness, hugs, tears, and this super cheesy redemption music. So 20 years later what does Danny Tanner have to do with writing a sales email and that's actually quite a lot. So in the final hours of your launch your prospect is likely to be feeling a combination of fear, anxiety around the deadline, self-judgment around not being in a position to pull the trigger sooner, and a host of just all this other stuff that's simply not conducive to the final conversion. So instead of procrastoshaming them and pouring the foam on even thicker, we actually exercise some of our, guess, our, get this, like our human capacities, our human faculties of empathy, you know, to acknowledge that this is all normal and congruent with what other people who have bought, you know, experienced as well as a natural part of that overall process. So we also contrast the instantly curable pain or resistance around the buying decision against the less curable pain and not deciding and resuming their old reality. So it's a lot more easily to reverse the buying decision. If they come into the program, they're not getting the results, it's just kind of not what they wanted, they could hit that undo button. If they don't buy the program, you know, and they resume their old reality, the one that really wasn't working for them, the one that wasn't serving and our program is no longer available, then that can't be reversed. So this creates an effect I call pain anchoring, the pain in buying versus the pain of not buying. And when you could anchor one against the other, it has a similar effect to price anchoring, making one seem a lot more valuable and welcoming than the other. So that's what we're going to be doing in this one. Finally, we'll build them back up by reminding our prospect of other wins and successes they've had and the positive qualities they've cultivated along the way. So we're really going to pump them up, show them that they do have these faculties and abilities lurking within them. It's just time to channel it and express it in order to take that next step. So that's it. We're going to cue the cheesy music right now and step into the theme plate itself. So we're going to start off by reversing the procrasta shame, show them it's okay they haven't enrolled yet, while also drawing attention to the deadline. So this is where you can get away with saying five hours left because you're not saying five hours left, you suck, why haven't you joined yet? You're actually being a lot more empathetic and grounded in the reality of the situation. You'll validate the resistance around investing. Sure, it's scary, I get it. Drop that guarantee, make it feel safer before you move forward in this email, then show that this resistance is still perfectly congruent with a buying behavior, maybe even a prerequisite for it. So don't exclude them because of this resistance. See if you can find a way to include them. Lead into a call to action and then start comparing the resistance in buying to the pain around the moment of highest tension that they'll continue to experience if they don't buy. So this is where we are anchoring the pain, making that fear and anxiety around the buying decision, pale in comparison to what's going to happen if they have to just resume that old reality. And that will set us up for another call to action. And then we could go into the qualities you admire or appreciate about them. So building them back up, showing them that they have these qualities inside of them, remind them of how those qualities have served them in the past and show them that those qualities will serve them now with the final call to action. So here is how it plays out. What's up, Jen? Five hours left and I notice you still haven't grabbed your seat inside the Andy yoga hustle masterclass. So I just wanted to check in and say, that's totally cool. So we're drawing attention to the deadline to create FOMO, but we're not agitating it. You know, people are agitated enough. They don't need us to agitate the anxiety around the FOMO any further. Instead, we validate it. You know, we validate that resistance instead of shaming it. Even with the 60 day W yoga rate guarantee and all, it's still a bigish decision. So dropping that guarantee. In fact, if I remember correctly, some of our best students waited till the final minutes before finally standing up and saying, I'm ready. So we're showing that this fear, this procrastination is perfectly congruent with being a buyer. Not only that, but they came in with a lot of stuff. Is this masterclass really worth the investment? Can I really double my rate just by taking an online class? Will those seven extra revenue streams I'll be learning about really work for me? What if I'm not tech savvy? Is this going to stretch me too far outside of my comfort zone? I don't want to be pushed here. I have to sell. I just want to chill out, flow with life and trust that it'll all work out. So really kind of framing that internal dialogue, that resistance, what it sounds like and showing that this is all congruent with being a buyer, if not a prerequisite for being a buyer. Sounding familiar yet? Here's the thing. You're not meant to join the program with your stuff all figured out. This is a place where you're invited to bring your stuff, your doubts, your fears, your sense that this will never work for me and simply surrender yourself to the proven roadmap that's been working for hundreds of yoga teachers from literally every corner of the world. So if you're ready to take the first step in ditching the yoga hustle, the running from studio to studio, ignoring the fact that your Uber bill is more than your hourly rate. So pain anchoring, that is part of that moment of highest tension. Secretly sharing the same doubts that your friends and family have expressed, although you'd never admit it to them out loud and feeling generally unyogic about the fact that some teachers make it while others don't. You know, that's a secret silent pain they have part of that moment of highest tension. Then I invite you to officially assume yoga crush status by taking this special eight week journey with me. So here's what we're doing. We've anchored the pain. You know, of course there is some doubts and fears and joining, but they're allowed to bring that stuff. That's inclusive. They're allowed to surrender it. And that's a lot easier than continuing on with the status quo and continuing to experience that moment of highest tension. So we've anchored that pain. Here's that call to action to join the program. And then this is where you can actually have your evergreen timers. Timers and scarcity and urgency aren't bad things. They're bad when, you know, they exist. And then you as a human with empathy who can coach a conversion decides to just reiterate what a timer says, you know, in that case, you're not adding any extra value. You're not actually coaching the conversion. You're not actually aware of the context in your prospect. You know, you don't need to scare your prospect into a sale. If you've done your job up to now, they're already fully aware of the consequences and the stakes and they've generated their own fear around it. Your job is to meet that and use that and alchemize that fear and anchor it against some other fears and coach them into that decisiveness. Even though the clock is ticking, here's why I'm not worried about you. You've been down this path before. When the whole world told you that you should keep playing it safe instead of venturing off to do this yoga thing, you still had the courage to pursue it. You know how flippin' rare and awesome that is. And even though you've had to contend with probing questions and veiled smiles every time the career conversation comes up, you still had to power on conviction to go with what in your heart you knew to be true. So now, even though I'm inviting you to take a scary step forward and redefining yourself and making this shift from underpaid, overworked yoga teacher to thriving and demanding yoga professional, I trust that you'll consciously choose what feels right in your heart. So we're empowering them to make this decision. We're showing them that they've done the scary thing before. They've gone against the grain. They've silenced the naysayers and the doubters and they've done it anyway. So this is within them. You know, they have the ability to do it. It's just time for them to express it and consciously choose. The only thing I ask is that you actually choose. So once again, binary choice. Let's do this. I'm a bit scared, but can't wait to give it a risk-free shot. So inclusive. They're still a little scared, but they could give it a risk-free shot. It's all about that scale, that balance, you know, with fears and resistance on one end and courage and excitement and safety on the other. We're not looking for it to be fully balanced on one side. We're looking for it there to just be enough courage and excitement and proof to weigh it down into the conversion versus, no, definitely not my vibe. Thanks, but no, thanks. Either way, it's been such a pleasure having you on this journey. If you make it into the masterclass, then I'll catch up with you a little later inside the Facebook group. Drop me a PM to let me know what you're most excited about. If not looking for it to find new ways to serve your awesome journey. So that's it. We're also closing with that hyper specific and vivid future pace of the ownership experience, making it feel safe, grounding it in what's actually going to happen. You know, they'll get their login details. I'll step into the Facebook group and exchange a few private messages, you know, on Facebook Messenger. That's a very familiar thing to this audience. So that is the Danny Tanner, a famous 90s dad coming back, not to haunt us, but to help us get higher conversions. So make good use of this one. Take about 20 to 25 minutes right now.
